item.aviator_dream.dehavilland_dh106.des:
The world's very first jet passenger airliner. Fitted with four centrifugal-flow jet engines, it flew fly faster than other propeller driven aircraft but also consumed more fuel.
 + This one got fuselage reinforcements so it won't repeat its history again.
item.aviator_dream.douglas_c47.descripti: A military version of the DC-3 focused on cargo transport refitted with a 14-cylinder engine. This thing can carry more than its previous type.
item.aviator_dream.douglas_dc1.descripti: A prototype airliner built by Douglas Aircraft that would continue its journey as the DC-2 and DC-3. It was reliable, fast, and able to carry a lot, but it was a little bit unstable.
item.aviator_dream.douglas_dc2.descripti: A production version of the DC-1. It was fitted with more seats and more powerful engines as well as some modifications to make it more stable.
item.aviator_dream.fokker_fviia.descript: A wooden airliner fitted with one 7-cylinder engine. It's slow and underpowered but was the best option for a starter airline owner back then.
item.aviator_dream.fokker_fviib3m.descri: An upgraded version of the F.VIIA with two more engines added for more reliability.
item.aviator_dream.lockheed_l1049g.descr: An airliner designed for long haul and high speed.
item.aviator_dream.test.description: A test entity used as a base for ground vehicles.
item.aviator_dream.toyota_stout_k100.des: A light truck fitted with a four-cylinder inline engine. Cheap and reliable, this thing will work for you as long as you feed it enough fuel. Just make sure to not drive it too fast.
